name: wretch (Tiny Fetch Wrapper with Middleware)

auth.notes: auth() and header() helpers; custom middleware for OAuth

agent_readiness.notes: Tiny fluent fetch wrapper with middleware and addons. import wretch from 'wretch'. const api = wretch('https://api.example.com').auth('Bearer TOKEN'). await api.get('/users').json(). await api.post({name: 'Alice'}).json(). await api.url('/users').get().json(). Error handling: .notFound(handler).unauthorized(handler). Addons: QueryStringAddon, AbortAddon, RetryAddon, PerformanceAddon. wretch.mix() for addon composition. Global: wretch.options({mode: 'cors'}). Catcher chain: .error(404, cb).error(401, cb).json(). FormData: .formData({file}). ArrayBuffer/blob responses. Dedupe addon for request deduplication. 2KB gzipped.

gotchas: Addon-based architecture — import addons individually to keep bundle small
Response type must be specified (.json(), .text(), .blob()) — no auto-detection
Error handling chain must be set up before terminal method (.json())
AbortAddon controller must be managed for timeout handling
best_when: Browser API clients needing fluent chainable API with small bundle
When axios-style interceptors are needed without axios's size
Progressive addon composition (retry, abort, dedup) as needed
avoid_when: Node.js heavy-lifting (use got or axios for streaming, retry, proxy)
When ofetch or native fetch is sufficient
summary: wretch is a tiny fluent fetch wrapper. wretch(url).auth('Bearer TOKEN').get('/path').json(). Per-status error handlers: .notFound(cb).unauthorized(cb). Addon system: RetryAddon, AbortAddon, DedupeAddon. 2KB gzipped. MIT by Julien Elbaz. Type-annotated. Best for browser API clients needing fluent API with small bundle. Response type must be explicit. Error handling before terminal. Addon must be imported for each capability.
